title: SSL Overlay
description: Reference schema for the Scheduling-Structural-Logical overlay (arXiv:2604.24026). Three optional fields annotate a SKILL.md frontmatter with preconditions, decomposition, and success criteria. This schema is NOT used to fail validation today — it is a reference document for validate.py and future tooling. Source-grounding rule: a field that is present must carry a real value. A value equal to 'null' means explicitly unknown (documented absence). A field that is absent means not yet annotated. A value matching TODO, FIXME, or angle-bracket placeholder (e.g. '<...>') is ungrounded and flagged as WARN by validate.py.

scheduling.description: Invocation-level attributes: preconditions and triggers that must hold before the skill is selected.

structural.description: Execution phases. Accepts a list of plain strings (simple form) or a list of typed-step objects (advanced form). Both shapes are valid during the 0.1-draft transition.

logical.description: Atomic success criterion: a measurable postcondition that defines 'done' for this skill.

typed-step.description: A single typed execution step within structural. The step label is required; actions, resources, and effects draw from closed draft enums (mismatch is INFO, not WARN, during 0.1-draft).

resources.description: Artifact types touched during this step.

effects.description: Observable outcomes produced by this step.

action-enum.description: Draft closed vocabulary for step actions. Mismatch against current free-text values is informational only during 0.1-draft.

resource-enum.description: Draft closed vocabulary for touched artifact types.

effect-enum.description: Draft closed vocabulary for observable outcomes.
